codeReview.summary: Implementation correctly adds limit enforcement with upgrade CTAs and the spikes upgrade CLI command. Worker returns 429 responses with upgrade_url field for SHARE_LIMIT and SPIKE_LIMIT errors. CLI upgrade command properly opens Stripe checkout. Tests verify both the 429 responses with upgrade_url and the checkout endpoint behavior.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The UPGRADE_URL constant is hardcoded as 'https://spikes.sh/pro' in multiple locations: worker/src/handlers/shares.ts, worker/src/handlers/spikes.ts, and implicitly in CLI error messages. AGENTS.md does not document this pattern for consistency.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: worker/src/handlers/shares.ts:14 defines UPGRADE_URL, worker/src/handlers/spikes.ts also references UPGRADE_URL, cli/src/error.rs:71-74 hardcodes URL in error messages

sharedStateObservations.observation: The feature implements STRIPE_PRO_PRICE_ID as an environment variable but there's no documentation in AGENTS.md or library files about required Stripe environment variables.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: worker/src/types.ts includes STRIPE_PRO_PRICE_ID in Env interface, worker/src/handlers/billing.ts checks for this variable, but no .factory/library file documents Stripe configuration

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker-backend skill procedure was followed correctly. Tests were written first (TDD pattern visible in transcript), migrations were not needed (reused existing schema), all tests pass, typecheck passes, and manual verification was done via wrangler dev.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows: created test file first, then implemented billing handler changes, verified with vitest run, tsc --noEmit, and wrangler dev health check

summary: Feature implementation is complete and correct. The limit enforcement returns 429 with upgrade_url for free users hitting share (5) and spike (1000) limits. The spikes upgrade CLI command successfully opens Stripe checkout. Tests cover the 429 response shape and checkout endpoint. No blocking issues found.
